item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The type of CC license was not clearly defined (meaning that the chance of it being NC or ND is equally as likely as BY-SA), and another source lists it down as All Rights Reserved. Due to this license uncertainty, it is assumed not compliant with CC BY-SA 3.0 and has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, what happens to removed instances of SCP-2080-1? Do they attempt to rejoin the group and do not much else?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You took the words right out of my mouth. I appreciate the subtle hints of this SPC, the parental regret, the initial creepiness of the house's effects, and the way the son has to deal with it all. I think it's a great example of an anomalous site that conveys a very simple, relatable message about family relationships while maintaining a seriously freaky edge. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot: it's bizarre and creepy and tragic all at once. The reason for the argument between 2080-1 and his son is a bit heavy-handed but I guess it works. Honestly, you could just say they had an argument that was more heated than normal, that would have been enough.

Something that really struck me about the snippets in Document 2080-02a was the implication that dad was often too busy for his kid, promising to be there but not fulfilling those promises. These naturally led me to think about all times I'd wished there were two of me, to fulfil obligations and responsibilities fully. Likewise, there are moments when I've made a decision and realized I wished I could see how things would have gone for me on the opposite side of that decision - I imagine a huge fight with a kid would be one of those times, but it seems seeing the alternative play out is not working out too well for Mr. Herschel…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoilers
– hide block

The sad thing is, he was trying to be. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Put 'Mr.' in front of the initial identification, so hopefully that works. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Went to bed last night and of course had a ton of ideas about this. Bolstered the containment procedures because that many dudes in one place talking all at once is gonna be audible outside. Also changed -1's name to hopefully drive home the family situation. Let me know if the interview formatting doesn't work.

Also also, I should thank djkaktus and KateMcTiriss, because his podcast got me back to wanting to write SCPs, and that episode is the one I was listening to while rewriting this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It says only ___ are animate but I don't necessarily know if that means some died, or what.

That to me indicated that there's a large number of corpses in there. That implication works far better than simply stating there's corpses in there, at least for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the creepiness of this, the fact that you keep getting clones of your parents just piling and piling in to your life, saying the same things over and over and over. Gives me the creeps. One thing I would add is a description of SCP-2080-1. I didn't know whether it was a male, female, or reptilian flying monkey. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for being less than a half hour from my house!

But seriously, this gets me in the feels. The image of a bunch of clones crammed into a house repeating the same lines is funny for a sec, then kind of dark, then sad. One thing though, the document doesn't say if these clones are subject to regular expiration from lack of food and water, crushing injuries, etc. It says only ___ are animate but I don't necessarily know if that means some died, or what.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Remember, be a responsible parent or you might get trampled by a horde of Eldritch shapeshifters all pretending to be you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Its very odd with the original being stuck in the house. I'd love to hear his interview, why they can't any more, and why he can't be rescued.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "In situ" means in its original place, so if they're containing it where they found it, it's the right term.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"—is contained in situ"

Is this supposed to be "on site"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Back in the saddle! I'm stuck on a reduplicated parents motif lately.

Image sourced from http://www.tikkun.org/nextgen/the-crisis-enters-year-five, licensed under Creative Commons.

Thanks to TwistedGears and vezaz for feedback.
